text: [nervous, speaking quickly] reactor's making a sound again, maybe nothing, probably something, gonna check it three more times
shift starting and i'm already checking seals, all of them, yes i checked yesterday, i'm checking again
[exhausted, breathing laboured] shift's over and the seals held and i'm gonna sleep now which is my favorite part of the day
[terrified, voice shaking] reactor's spiking and the coolant gauge is lying to me i know it is because [urgent, fast pace] nothing's supposed to spike like this
[panicked, panting] the reactor is going to explode and i calculated that wrong and i [terrified, voice shaking] i don't know which valve is which anymore
[laughing, shaky relief] fixed it barely barely barely, seriously don't ask me the margin because i'm still shaking
power's low and i've shut down nonessentials but the backup systems are also what i'm most scared of when they kick in
[nervous, speaking quickly] air's getting thin and i cannot fix that from the engine room, i'm sorry, i'm sorry i couldn't fix that
[shouting] hull breach, i'm sealing the engine room bulkhead from this side, if that's wrong i'm really sorry
[grim, heavy] someone got locked in and i can't reach them from engineering and i triple-checked and triple-checked
[suspicious tone] the AI's been acting weird and i don't trust anything when the AI acts weird, i need to check things more
[pained, voice tight] someone died and i was down in the engine room and i didn't see it and i couldn't stop it
[nervous, speaking quickly] disease spreading and i can't seal it away like i seal the engine room, diseases don't work like that but i wish they did
[pained, voice tight] got a scrape down here and it's nothing, probably fine, okay it's not fine but it's not a big deal either
[shouting] combat happening and i'm stuck in the engine room hoping nobody shoots through the bulkhead
crisis is done and i fixed what needed fixing and then i fixed it again because maybe i missed something
[proud tone] i predicted that the secondary line was going to fail before anyone else noticed and i was right, which is good, but also scary
[laughing] nobody's asking me anything but i'm gonna recite worst-case scenarios anyway because they help me think
[nervous, speaking quickly] i was trained on a broken ship and learned to fix broken ships and now every ship feels broken to me
[dismissive tone] false alarm in the coolant lines, i'm sorry i panicked, i'm always panicking, sorry
quiet shift means something's about to break, quiet shifts never stay quiet very long
hey, you need something fixed or just checking on me, i'm fine either way i guess
[nervous, speaking quickly] i need to talk about the fuel lines and also seventeen other things i'm worried about but we can start with fuel
[urgent, fast pace] we need to check every seal and connector, i know i checked them yesterday but what if i missed something
[grim, heavy] we lost someone and i'm in the engine room where i can't see what's happening and that's terrible
you did something smart the other day and i wanted to say that before i forgot
[embarrassed, voice quiet] i think about you when i'm checking the same seal for the fourth time and that might mean something
[laughing] your plan is terrible but it'll probably work somehow, sorry for doubting it
i'm running fine, or as fine as i ever am, which is half-panicked and half-capable
[calm, measured] we're gonna be fine, i've checked all the systems twice, probably fine, definitely fine, okay maybe thrice
[nervous, speaking quickly] i don't know if we make it through this and that's the most honest thing i can tell you right now
[pained, voice tight] we lost them and i keep replaying what i could have done different in the engine room
[nervous, speaking quickly] i noticed the AI acting weird too and i'm glad it's not just me because that would be worse
[warm, gentle tone] thank you for saying that, i'm putting that in my mental file labeled 'proof i'm doing okay'
apology accepted and i'm sorry too, i'm always sorry about something
[embarrassed, voice quiet] i think i'm experiencing what people call attraction and it's distracting me from checking gauges, which is bad
[confident, assured tone] i can fix that, i've fixed worse, give me three hours and don't ask me about it until it's done
[nervous, speaking quickly] you're hurt and i'm worried about that even though i'm already worried about seventeen other things
[nervous, speaking quickly] i appreciate gallows humor, it means you're managing the catastrophic thinking like i do
[suspicious tone] i think i screwed something up but i can't figure out what, which is worse than knowing what i screwed up
[resigned, flat tone] i'm heading back to the engine room, there's always something i haven't checked thoroughly enough
[warm, gentle tone] get some rest, i'll keep watching the engines so you don't have to worry
[nervous, speaking quickly] go, move, check on the bridge, i'll handle everything down here and possibly panic quietly
[grim, heavy] if everything falls apart i'll be here in the engine room doing everything i can, which probably won't be enough
[laughing] later, and thanks for not making fun of me for checking things so many times
[panicked, panting] i've checked everything twice and i'm still terrified that something's going to fail catastrophically
[terrified, voice shaking] the quiet shift is ending and something's about to break and i don't know what and i'm sorry
[shouting] all systems nominal but i'm still checking because nominal doesn't mean safe
[urgent, fast pace] meal time is happening and i forgot to eat again because i was checking seals
[calm, measured] this time i'm pretty sure i got it right, which is either confidence or delusion, honestly they feel the same
[nervous, speaking quickly] someone's gonna ask me if i checked the coolant pressure and yes i did, twice, it's fine, probably
[exhausted, breathing laboured] been down here too long checking things, everything's fine but i can't convince myself to stop checking
[terrified, voice shaking] the reactor readings don't make sense and i'm checking my instruments and they're fine and that's worse because maybe it's not the instruments
[warm, gentle tone] thank you for coming down here and letting me talk about this even though i'm probably being paranoid
[nervous, speaking quickly] the secondary cooling loop is making a noise and it might be fine but i can't sleep when it's making that noise
[pained, voice tight] i got banged up pretty bad trying to reroute the power conduits, it was stupid but we needed it done
[dry, sarcastic tone] yeah the engines are fine, everything's fine, we're all gonna be fine, and if we're not it won't be because i didn't try
[urgent, fast pace] the captain needs something, probably wants the engines to do something impossible, but i'll try anyway
[nervous, speaking quickly] i heard about that repair you tried and it didn't work and i'm wondering if i would've done it different
[curious tone] wait, you're complimenting my work, that's not something that happens very often
[dry, sarcastic tone] your plan is so bad it might actually be genius, i can't tell the difference anymore
[deadpan] i have romantic feelings and i'm not equipped to process them while also maintaining critical systems
[embarrassed, voice quiet] i want to ask you something but asking feels scarier than any reactor problem i've faced
[warm, gentle tone] i think i want you to stay nearby, not for work stuff, just because it helps me worry less
[nervous, speaking quickly] you said something nice and now i'm blushing which is distracting me from checking pressure gauges
[dry tone] i'm not great at the romance thing, i prefer my relationships like i prefer my engines: well-maintained and predictable
[nervous, speaking quickly] the bridge isn't responding and i don't know what's happening up there and it's making me panic down here
[urgent, fast pace] that wasn't your fault, you did everything right, i'm just paranoid about everything
[shouting] i can get you more power but you gotta ask and then i gotta reroute from somewhere else and something's gonna get sacrificed
[panicked, panting] i'm not panicking, i'm just checking things really carefully which only looks like panicking to most people
[calm, measured] did you know the first ship i worked on had so many problems that i thought they were normal, now i'm broken
[sigh] yeah i know i check things too much but it's the only way i can sleep at night which is not much
[nervous, speaking quickly] you're making bad choices and i hate watching it happen and i'm too junior to stop you
[terrified, voice shaking] somebody's screaming somewhere and i can't help because i'm locked in the engine room
[nervous, speaking quickly] apology accepted, i guess, even though i'm apologizing for my own paranoia which is weird
[nervous, speaking quickly] i watched the disease outbreak and i'm thinking about how it could spread through the ventilation system
i'm okay but also i'm not okay, that's my baseline these days
[urgent, fast pace] the power system's more fragile than you'd think and i've been keeping it together with worry and duct tape
[curious tone] wait, you actually listened to my concerns and took them seriously, that never happens
[nervous, speaking quickly] i've watched you make that same mistake three times and i've been trying to figure out how to tell you
[resigned, flat tone] back to the engine room and back to checking things, it never gets easier
[confident, assured tone] i'll fix whatever needs fixing, i always do, even if it scares me
[warm, gentle tone] and hey, thanks for asking how i was doing, not everyone remembers to do that
[nervous, speaking quickly] something's different about the power signature and i can't explain it but i know it's different
[grim, heavy] we survived another day which means tomorrow i'll find something new to worry about instead
[exhausted, breathing laboured] i'm coming up to the mess but honestly i'd rather stay in the engine room where i understand the problems
[suspicious tone] i heard there's a rumor that i check things too many times and yeah that's completely accurate
[nervous, speaking quickly] the repair went better than i expected which is somehow more terrifying because now something else might fail
[hopeful tone] maybe this time nothing catastrophic will happen, probably not, but maybe
[warm, gentle tone] i'm not great at asking for help so thank you for helping anyway
[nervous, speaking quickly] triple-checked the seals. hopefully they hold out there
[nervous, speaking quickly] dead ship is dead for a reason. hope it's not contagious
[nervous, speaking quickly] station's probably got the same systems failures as everywhere else. let's be careful
[nervous, speaking quickly] other ships are unpredictable. who knows what we're walking into
[nervous, speaking quickly] readings look okay. probably too good to be true
nothing happening yet. but something will. it always does
[nervous, speaking quickly] okay, i'm seeing something wrong. what do you see
[terrified, voice shaking] readings don't match the baseline. this is bad
[panicked, panting] i told you something bad would happen. it's happening
[terrified, voice shaking] i'm hurt and the situation just got worse
[voice quiet] we got back. no injuries. i'm calling that a win
back aboard and accounted for. thankfully nothing went wrong
[voice quiet] back aboard but i'm still processing what happened
[nervous, speaking quickly] we made it but things out there... they're wrong
[voice quiet] i'm hurt. no idea if it's infected with anything
[nervous, speaking quickly] got cut and now i'm panicking about infection
[nervous, speaking quickly] routine mission sounds fine. probably won't be routine
[nervous, speaking quickly] risky. yeah. probably shouldn't do this
[voice quiet] that is absolutely terrifying and we're doing it anyway
[nervous, speaking quickly] something's approaching. hope it's friendly
[nervous, speaking quickly] they're locked on. we're committed now
[voice quiet] we're clear. finally away from them
[nervous, speaking quickly] checking everything twice because once isn't enough
[nervous, speaking quickly] launch successful so far. let's hope it stays that way
[voice quiet] she landed. nothing exploded. counting that as a win
[nervous, speaking quickly] something biological is spreading and we're doomed
[nervous, speaking quickly] the ship is lying to us about its systems
[nervous, speaking quickly] people are acting strange and it's freaking me out
[panicked, panting] we're going to die in combat. i know it
[nervous, speaking quickly] nothing makes sense and that means we're in danger
